behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Start at Unit. The failure is local lint logic over template text (detecting copied phase instructions). Unit -> Integration is not proven because the behavior is explicitly a static lint check, not a cross-module runtime handoff. Higher levels rejected: no real OS/network/subprocess dependency in the behavior text, no model-output dependency, and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Test the lint function with template fixtures: one containing verbatim/near-verbatim phase instructions from SKILL.md (must fail) and one clean template (must pass). Include normalization edge cases (whitespace/casing) to catch bypasses.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation/fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Unit -> Integration proof: leakage can be introduced at module handoff during interpolation/fixture assembly even if the template itself is clean. Integration-brake checks: no required real network/subprocess/OS primitive beyond normal in-process assembly; no AI-output dependence; no multi-step agent chain. Higher levels rejected because the behavior text is about assembled prompt content before model execution.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt-construction path (template + fixture interpolation + harness assembly) and assert the final prompt has no banned phase-instruction spans derived from SKILL.md.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from the plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: Unit -> Integration proof: the failure boundary is interface handoff between invocation code, skill-loader, and prompt builder (source-of-truth selection). Integration-brake: this can be caught with local filesystem/module wiring; behavior text does not require real external HTTP/subprocess, real model-quality evaluation, or multi-agent sequencing. Higher levels rejected because the key check is provenance of context (file-loaded vs hard-coded), not model quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute invocation against a fixture plugin/skill directory and assert the loaded context hash/content is included in the test path, while hard-coded phase rules are absent. Fail if behavior passes when file-loaded context is removed.

behaviors.description: If the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails with the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Unit -> Integration proof: requires harness loading changed skill content and propagating it into invocation. Integration -> System proof: requires real model/API execution path for the behavioral test outcome. System -> Agentic proof: correctness depends on model output quality under changed skill context; deterministic stubs can falsely pass and hide regression. Workflow rejected: behavior describes a single user-style prompt evaluation, not multiple agentic steps in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run A/B behavioral smoke test with identical user-style prompt: baseline SKILL.md vs degraded/removed target section. Call the real model and require statistically reliable degradation/failure signal (e.g., repeated trials with threshold) tied to structured assertions.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: Unit -> Integration proof: failure appears at ordering boundary between model-response handler, artifact writer, and parser/extractor modules. Integration-brake: no need for real network/subprocess/OS-specific behavior; a deterministic model stub is sufficient to prove ordering and artifact persistence. Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior is pipeline ordering, not model-quality correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Drive the response-processing pipeline with a stub raw model payload; assert artifact write occurs first and persists exact raw text, then parsing runs. Include malformed JSON case to confirm raw artifact still exists when parsing fails.

behaviors.description: Assertions verify structured behavior (for example field placement or schema shape), not mere keyword presence that could come from prompt echoing.
behaviors.justification: Start at Unit. This behavior is primarily assertion-logic correctness (schema/field-shape checks vs keyword match). Unit -> Integration not required unless proving cross-module extraction wiring; the behavior text focuses on assertion criterion itself. Higher levels rejected: no required real OS/network/subprocess, no required model-quality dependence, no multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Test assertion helpers with synthetic outputs: (a) keyword-echo text lacking required structure (must fail), (b) correctly structured output with minimal keywords (must pass), (c) wrong field placement/type (must fail).
